policy.title: RBI finalizes climate stress testing guidelines for scheduled commercial banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines requiring all scheduled commercial banks to conduct annual climate stress tests starting FY2027, with mandatory scenario analysis covering both physical and transition risks. Banks must report climate-adjusted PD/LGD metrics for top 100 exposures by asset value.

policy.title: SEBI mandates BRSR Core assurance for top 500 listed companies
policy.description: SEBI extended mandatory reasonable assurance requirements for BRSR Core disclosures to top 500 companies by market capitalization, effective FY2027. The circular emphasizes Scope 3 emissions reporting and water stress metrics aligned with ISSB standards.

policy.title: IRDAI releases draft framework for parametric climate insurance products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft guidelines enabling insurers to offer parametric products for flood, drought, and cyclone perils with pre-approved trigger indices. The framework allows banks and NBFCs to distribute these products as credit-linked insurance for agricultural and MSME portfolios.

policy.title: Basel Committee publishes Pillar 3 climate disclosure standards
policy.description: BCBS finalized Pillar 3 climate-related financial risk disclosures with implementation deadline of January 2028. Standards require banks to disclose financed emissions, physical risk exposures by geography, and climate-adjusted credit risk metrics.

policy.title: EU adopts delegated acts for sector-specific ESRS standards
policy.description: European Commission adopted sector-specific sustainability reporting standards for financial institutions under CSRD, including detailed requirements for financed emissions and climate risk exposure disclosures. Indian companies with EU subsidiaries face compliance requirements from FY2027.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk pathways
policy.description: NGFS published updated climate scenarios incorporating latest IPCC AR6 data, with new granular physical risk pathways for South Asia including compound flood-heat events. Scenarios now extend to 2100 with decadal breakpoints for financial planning.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide climate risk scoring for the bank's commercial real estate and infrastructure portfolio. The deal includes integration of Jupiter's ClimateScore platform into HDFC's credit underwriting workflow.

competitors.title: MSCI launches India-specific climate value-at-risk module
competitors.description: MSCI ESG introduced a dedicated India CVaR module covering 1,200 listed companies with district-level physical hazard overlays. The product integrates monsoon variability and heat stress projections calibrated to Indian meteorological data.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $12M Series A to expand BRSR compliance platform
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed a $12M Series A led by Accel India to scale its ESG reporting and BRSR compliance platform. The company plans to add climate risk modules and Scope 3 calculation tools for mid-market enterprises.

competitors.title: XDI releases asset-level physical risk API for emerging market banks
competitors.description: XDI launched a new API product targeting banks in Asia-Pacific, offering asset-level physical risk scores across eight perils including riverine flood, coastal inundation, and extreme heat. Pricing starts at $50K annually for portfolio analysis.

competitors.title: Moody's partners with CRISIL on India climate credit assessments
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions announced a data partnership with CRISIL to integrate India-specific physical risk metrics into credit assessments for Indian corporates. The collaboration covers 500 rated entities across infrastructure, power, and real estate sectors.

competitors.title: CarbonMint selected by SBI for agricultural carbon credit verification
competitors.description: CarbonMint was chosen by State Bank of India to provide MRV services for the bank's agricultural carbon finance program across 50,000 smallholder farmers. The platform will track sequestration and issue verified carbon credits linked to SBI's green loan products.

research.title: Climate-Adjusted Credit Risk: A Framework for Emerging Market Banks
research.description: IFC study proposes methodology for integrating physical climate risks into PD/LGD models for banks in South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa. Analysis of 2,000 loan defaults found physical hazard exposure increases default probability by 18-34% for agricultural and real estate sectors.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in India: Market Assessment and Product Design
research.description: Swiss Re estimates India's parametric insurance market could reach $2.5 billion by 2030, with credit-linked products for MSMEs representing 40% of potential premium. Report identifies flood and drought as highest-demand perils with 72% of surveyed banks expressing product interest.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Investor Survey and Recommendations
research.description: Survey of 85 institutional investors found 67% cite data quality and coverage as primary barriers to ESG integration in emerging market portfolios. India ranked third-highest priority for improved ESG data infrastructure after China and Brazil.

research.title: Physical Risk and Bank Lending: Evidence from Indian Districts
research.description: RBI research paper analyzing 15 years of district-level credit data found banks reduced lending by 8-12% in districts experiencing consecutive extreme weather events. Study recommends climate-adjusted provisioning and risk-based pricing frameworks.

research.title: Index-Based Insurance for Climate Adaptation: Global Stocktake
research.description: Munich Re's global review of parametric insurance programs identifies basis risk and index design as key challenges, with recommended correlation thresholds of 85%+ between trigger and actual loss. Report highlights successful bank-insurer partnerships in Kenya and Philippines as models.

research.title: Landscape of Climate Finance in India 2025
research.description: CPI tracks $68 billion in climate finance flows to India in 2025, a 23% increase year-on-year, with 78% directed to mitigation. Report identifies significant data gaps in tracking adaptation finance and private sector climate investments.

ourRead.headline: RBI's climate stress testing mandate creates an 18-month window for StepChange to become the default PD/LGD engine for Indian banks—but global players are already at the gates.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: Indian financial regulators are accelerating climate risk requirements faster than banks can build internal capacity, creating urgent demand for exactly what StepChange offers. The RBI stress testing guidelines, SEBI BRSR expansion, and IRDAI parametric framework form a regulatory trifecta that validates our three strategic bets simultaneously.

The RBI's finalized stress testing guidelines (policy-1) are the most consequential development. The mandate for climate-adjusted PD/LGD metrics on top 100 exposures by FY2027 creates an immediate compliance need that banks cannot meet with spreadsheets. Combined with the RBI research paper (research-4) documenting 8-12% lending reductions in climate-affected districts, regulators are signaling they want quantified, auditable climate risk integration—not checkbox exercises. StepChange should position our PD/LGD models as regulator-ready from day one.

The Jupiter-HDFC partnership (comp-1) is a wake-up call. A global climate risk player has landed a flagship Indian client, proving that private banks will pay for physical risk analytics when regulatory pressure mounts. However, Jupiter's product is designed for developed markets; the IFC study (research-1) showing 18-34% PD increases from physical hazards in South Asia suggests calibration for Indian conditions is critical. StepChange's local presence and India-specific hazard data remain our moat—but we need visible client wins to counter Jupiter's brand credibility.

The Swiss Re parametric insurance report (research-2) quantifies our second bet: a $2.5 billion market opportunity with 72% of banks interested in flood and drought products. IRDAI's draft framework (policy-3) explicitly enables bank-distributed parametric products. StepChange should immediately engage IRDAI during the comment period and position ourselves as the technical partner for trigger index design. The CarbonMint-SBI partnership (comp-6) shows state banks are willing to work with Indian startups on climate-linked products.

One thing to watch: MSCI's India CVaR module (comp-2) represents a direct threat to our Global South data positioning—global incumbents are localizing faster than expected. One action for this week: schedule conversations with three mid-sized private banks to pilot stress testing modules before Jupiter expands its HDFC relationship into a platform play.
